headline: ConocoPhillips and Loar added to Goldman Sachs conviction list
summary: Goldman Sachs has shaken up its US “conviction list – directors’ cut”, adding oil major ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP, XETRA:YCP) and aerospace supplier Loar Holdings (NYSE:LOAR), while removing Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NYSE:HII), Valero Energy Corp (NYSE:VLO) and Madison Square Garden...

headline: Cheniere, Exxon, Chevron Stocks Rise Again. The Energy Shock Is Upending Markets.
summary: Energy stocks were surging ahead of the bell Tuesday, with oil and gas prices soaring as fighting in the Middle East showed no signs of slowing.  Shares in oil exploration and production companies rallied again ahead of the bell—  ConocoPhillips  and  Occidental Petroleum  were both up by more than 3%.  Exxon Mobil  and  Chevron  were also higher.

headline: Is ConocoPhillips (COP) Using Permian Sales To Quietly Redraw Its Capital Returns Playbook?
summary: In February 2026, media reports said ConocoPhillips began exploring a sale of selected Permian Basin assets, potentially raising about US$2.00 billion as part of a broader portfolio streamlining that includes an expanded divestiture program. At the same time, the company attracted fresh analyst attention for an expected upswing in free cash flow as major projects such as Willow move closer to completion, reinforcing investor focus on its capital returns profile. We’ll now examine how...

headline: Iran, Dip-Buyers & a Rare Buffett Stumble: Wall Street Shakes Off Its Worst Fears
summary: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran rattled markets at the open, but seasoned dip-buyers pushed the major indices back to near-flat by the close. Meanwhile, Berkshire Hathaway's post-Buffett era gets off to a rocky start, and energy and defense stocks surge.
